serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle or can of packaged black tea is approximately 250 ml (250 g), providing about 75 kcal and 18.5 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Black Tea, Ready-to-Drink, Sweetened — HPB Singapore Foodo / Nutrient Composition of Foods in Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for packaged sweetened black tea (cr_id D09020016). All core macros and secondary nutrients confirmed from this source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific packaged sweetened black tea product. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the GI of sucrose (GI ~65) and diluted sugar-sweetened beverages, referencing the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugar-sweetened soft drinks and tea-based beverages. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 18.475) / 100 ≈ 10.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.29 g sugar per 100 ml; exceeds the 5 g/100 ml SSB threshold for hypertension risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage; despite low sodium (8 mg/100 ml), the added sugar load (7.29 g/100 ml) independently elevates blood pressure risk through insulin-driven sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation (AHA 2018); replace with unsweetened black tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage; 7.29 g free sugar per 100 ml with zero dietary fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: This product is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes; liquid sugar is absorbed without fibre buffering, causing acute postprandial glucose elevation; all sugar-sweetened beverages should be eliminated from the diabetic diet and replaced with unsweetened tea or water.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk via dyslipidaemia, visceral adiposity, and chronic low-grade inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages including this product; habitual SSB consumption raises triglycerides and promotes visceral fat accumulation; switching to unsweetened black tea preserves the cardiovascular-protective polyphenol benefit without the sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (7.29 g/100 ml) with zero fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful anti-inflammatory factor present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This product is unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; the added sugar load promotes inflammatory cytokine activity and the packaged formulation provides no compensating anti-inflammatory polyphenols, omega-3, or fibre; choose unsweetened brewed black tea to obtain theaflavin benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Packaged sweetened black tea is a ready-to-drink (RTD) beverage consisting of brewed black tea with added sugar, sold in bottles or cans. It is widely available across Singapore and Southeast Asia as a convenient everyday drink. The flavour profile is mildly astringent and sweet, with the characteristic tannin notes of black tea softened by the sugar content. Unlike freshly brewed unsweetened black tea, the packaged variant contains approximately 7.3 g of free sugar per 100 ml, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by standard public health definitions. Per 100 ml it provides 30 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 7.39 g carbohydrate (of which 7.29 g is sugar), with negligible dietary fibre and low sodium (8 mg). Clinically, this product is classified as an SSB and is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to the rapid glycaemic impact of liquid sugar and the absence of fibre buffering. The polyphenol content of packaged sweetened tea is insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory sugar load relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. A typical single-serve bottle of 250 ml delivers approximately 75 kcal and 18.5 g of sugar per serving. Consumers seeking the health benefits of black tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins) should choose unsweetened variants.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020016, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from this exact entry. GI is an analogous estimate documented with approved source reference.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo: this is a water-based sugar-sweetened beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo: the product contains no fat-bearing ingredients by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo: brewed tea with sugar contains no dietary fibre. saturated_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged RTD black tea product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sugar-sweetened beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this generic commercial beverage product with no specific cultural or regional cuisine origin; local_names fields are set to null accordingly.
